option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# AGENTS.md - AI Agent Guide for Homework Project
## Project Overview This is an educational Java project containing 8 homework assignments demonstrating fundamental programming concepts. Each assignment is organized in a separate package (`homework1` through `homework8`) with no external dependencies—pure Java using only the JDK standard library.
## Architecture & Package Structure
**Key Principle**: Each homework package is self-contained and demonstrates a specific concept progression.
- `homework1/` - Basic arithmetic calculations (H1_Calculator, H2_SumOfDigits, etc.) - `homework2/` - Conditional logic with Scanner input (NumberChecker, TemperatureChecker) - `homework3/` - Array operations and console visualization (ArrayTasks, AsteriskTask) - `homework4/` - Game logic with state management (TicTacToe game with winner detection) - `homework5Task1/` - Object-oriented basics (CreditCard class with methods, encapsulation) - `homework5Task2/` - ATM simulation with state - `homework6Task1/` - Inheritance & polymorphism (Doctor base class, Therapist/Surgeon/Dentist subclasses, pattern-matching in assignDoctor method) - `homework8/` - String manipulation (StringBuilder usage for efficient concatenation, Scanner for input)
## Critical Patterns & Conventions
### Naming Convention - **Class files use numbered prefix**: `H1_Calculator`, `Task1StringLength` (not camelCase filenames) - **Package names**: `homeworkN`, `homeworkNTaskN` (follow pattern exactly) - **Method names**: follow standard Java camelCase (e.g., `displayInfo()`, `checkWin()`)
### Entry Points & Main Methods - Every executable class has a `main(String[] args)` method - Homework packages often have a `Main.java` or named class as entry point - Complex programs (homework4: TicTacToe, homework6Task1: Clinic) use their named class as entry point
### Common Implementation Patterns
**State Management** (homework4 - TicTacToe): - Static board array for game state - Static currentPlayer flag for turn tracking - Game loop with state checks (checkWin, isDraw) ```java private static char[][] board = new char[SIZE][SIZE]; private static char currentPlayer = 'X'; while (gameRunning) { /* state updates */ } ```
**Object Encapsulation** (homework5Task1 - CreditCard): - Package-private fields (no access modifiers = package scope) - Methods for state manipulation (deposit, withdraw) - Display methods for output ```java String accountNumber; // package-private void deposit(double amount) { /* modify balance */ } ```
**Inheritance & Polymorphism** (homework6Task1): - `Doctor` base class with method overloading (`treat()`, `treat(String patientName)`) - Subclasses: `Therapist`, `Surgeon`, `Dentist` override base methods - Pattern: Clinic uses `Therapist.assignDoctor(Patient)` with switch/if-else logic based on patient.treatmentPlan
**String Processing** (homework8): - Heavy use of `StringBuilder` for efficient concatenation in loops - `Scanner` for user input with `nextLine()`, `nextInt()` - Step-by-step output for educational visibility
### Console I/O Patterns - Extensive use of `System.out.println()` for debugging/output - Russian language output (кириллица) is standard - User prompts use colon syntax: `"Введи номер строки (1-3): "` - Status headers: `"=== НАЗВАНИЕ ==="` format for section separation
### Method Organization - Helper methods placed after main logic - Methods named by responsibility (initBoard, printBoard, makeMove, checkWin) - No JavaDoc comments; inline explanations use Russian language comments
## Build & Execution
**IDE**: IntelliJ IDEA (Homework.iml module file present) - Right-click Main class → Run 'ClassName.main()' - Or use default Java compilation: `javac src/homeworkN/*.java` then `java -cp src homeworkN.ClassName`
**Key Build Files**: - `Homework.iml` - IntelliJ module configuration (inherits JDK, src/ as source folder) - `.gitignore` - Standard Java/IntelliJ ignores (out/, .idea/)
## Testing & Validation
- **No unit tests** - all validation is manual console output verification - Test via running main() and checking console output matches expected behavior - Example (homework4): Run TicTacToe, check that winner detection works for rows/columns/diagonals
## Common Workflows for AI Agents
1. **Adding a new homework**: Create `homeworkN/` package, add numbered class files, ensure main() exists 2. **Fixing logic bugs**: Check state management first (static variables in game logic), then algorithm correctness 3. **Implementing inheritance**: Follow Doctor/Therapist/Surgeon pattern—base class with protected fields, subclass override, parent class contains polymorphic dispatch 4. **String/Array operations**: Prefer StringBuilder over String concatenation in loops (homework8 example)
## Integration & Dependencies
- **Zero external dependencies** - pure JDK only - `java.util.Scanner` for all user input - `java.lang.StringBuilder` for string building - No third-party libraries or frameworks
## Important Edge Cases & Gotchas
- **Scanner not closed**: Some classes forget `scanner.close()` — check for resource leaks - **Static state in games**: TicTacToe reuses static board—calling main() twice will keep old board state - **Package-private fields**: CreditCard uses no access modifier (package-private), not private - **Method overloading vs. overriding**: homework6 uses both (overload treat() in Doctor, override in subclasses) - **Array bounds**: Game/array logic must validate indices (homework4 checks row/col bounds)
## Code Style Notes
- No explicit access modifiers on class fields = package-private (intentional design choice) - Heavy console output for educational purposes (shows step-by-step execution) - Russian comments and strings throughout (cultural localization) - Minimal separation of concerns—logic often mixed with I/O in single class
